title: WHO-Based Physical Activity and Exercise Guidance
context: Detailed Physical Exercise and Activity Guidelines for Indian and South Indian Adults
version: RAG Injection Knowledge Base – Physical Activity Module

based_on: WHO Global Action Plan on Physical Activity
Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) Lifestyle Guidelines
National Programme for Prevention and Control of Cancer, Diabetes, Cardiovascular Diseases and Stroke (NPCDCS)
content.core_principle: Physical activity is essential for prevention and control of diabetes, obesity, cardiovascular disease, hypertension, metabolic syndrome, depression, and premature mortality.

sections.section_title: Definition of Physical Activity vs Exercise

content.physical_activity_definition: Any bodily movement produced by skeletal muscles that requires energy expenditure.
content.exercise_definition: Structured, planned, repetitive physical activity aimed at improving or maintaining physical fitness.

sections.section_title: WHO Recommended Activity Levels for Adults (18–64 years)

content.muscle_strengthening: At least 2 days per week involving major muscle groups
content.additional_benefits: More than 300 minutes moderate activity per week provides additional health benefits
content.reduce_sedentary_time: Limit prolonged sitting and replace sedentary time with light physical activity.

moderate_activity.description: Noticeable increase in heart rate and breathing

sections.section_title: Physical Activity for Diabetes Prevention and Control

recommended_for_diabetics.avoid: Skipping exercise for more than 2 consecutive days

clinical_effect.weight_loss_effect: 5–7% weight loss reduces diabetes risk significantly

sections.section_title: Step Count Guidance for Indian Adults

content.indian_recommendation: Minimum 8000–10000 steps per day for metabolic health improvement

sections.section_title: Yoga and Traditional Indian Physical Practices
Sedentary Lifestyle Risks in Indian Urban Population

content.recommendation: Same as adults but adjusted to ability
avoid: Sudden high-intensity workouts without medical clearance

sections.section_title: Weekly Structured Exercise Plan Example (Indian Context)

content.problem_statement: Rapid urbanization and sedentary occupations have reduced daily physical activity in Indian populations.
content.key_issue: South Indians have higher central obesity and insulin resistance at lower BMI levels.
content.solution: Daily structured exercise combined with reduced sedentary time is essential to prevent diabetes and cardiovascular disease.
